dialogos.texto_en: Finally, we've arrived at the hotel,\nUesugi-san.
I'm really tired...
I agree.\nShall we check-in early and rest up?
Alright, let's enjoy sightseeing to the fullest\nstarting from tomorrow! I'm so excited!
...Wait a second.\nAre we really staying here?
Is there a problem?
A problem...
(The atmosphere in this lobby,\n it's clearly not the world I know.)
(With various restaurants offering Japanese,\n Western and Chinese cuisine,\n a fitness center, and a fully equipped indoor pool.)
(And there's even a lounge on the top floor\n that boasts a night view? This hotel seems to\n go beyond the level of a high school graduation trip.)
W-well, ya know?\nIsn't this a luxury hotel with a super high price tag?
Well, I guess it is.\nDo you like it, Fu-kun?
...I left the travel plan to you guys, but\nis it really okay to stay in a place like this?
We're the ones who decided on Okinawa,\nso you don't have to worry about it, Futaro.
Ah!\nAre you perhaps worried about the travel expenses?
Now that you mention it, you're gonna pay us back\nafter you become successful, right?
We'll have to ensure he earns a lot in the future.
You know, there's a limit\nto how much one can pay back.
Futaro, you'll be fine.\nYou'll surely pay it back in no time.
You guys...
You don't have to push yourself too hard, you know?
No, I'll pay! Definitely!\nI don't want to owe you guys anything extra!
...But, could you extend the deadline a little bit?
Aren't you stubborn.
We've already come this far, so I can't back down!\nAlright, let's stay here!
That's the spirit, Uesugi-san!
Now that Futaro-kun has made up his mind,\nhow should we assign the rooms?\nLet's decide before check-in.
By the way, how many rooms did we reserve?
I think it was four twin rooms.
Then, since there are six of us%p-2;――
I'll share a room with Fu-kun.\nIs that okay?
Huh?
It's not okay.\nBecause Futaro will be sharing a room with me.
No way!\nWait a minute, both of you.\nThat's really not appropriate, now.
What's not appropriate?\nI don't mind in the slightest.
I just want to be with the person I like.\nI don't think there's anything wrong with that.
A-as I said, that kinda thing...
Fu-kun, you'd have so much fun rooming with me.\nCome on, no good?
Futaro, stay with me, okay?
(What's with Nino and Miku being so proactive?\n If we actually end up sharing a room...)
Ummm... Maybe it's not the best idea \nto share a room with Uesugi-san?
Look, doesn't Dad use this hotel a lot?
If he were to find out\nthat the two of you stayed together,\nthings won't go so smoothly for Futaro-kun.
Besides, logically,\nyou should stay in a single room, right,\nFutaro-kun?
That's right!
Well, let's decide then.\nWe can't keep arguing like this forever.
Alright, then let's get things settled, Ichika.
Hmm, I get it.
I'll stay in a room with Itsuki-chan.\nIs that okay with you?
I don't mind.
Then it's just the three of us left.\nNino, Miku, what do you want to do?
We need to decide soon,\nor we won't be able to check in.
Well, I don't have a choice.\nI'll room alone.\nIt's more convenient for me.
Then it's decided.\nMiku and I will share a room. I'm looking forward to it.
Sorry, Yotsuba.\nI also want to room alone.
Huh?\nThen what will happen to the room I'm staying in?
Stay at Nino's.
Why do I have to do that?\nCan't you stay with Yotsuba in the same room?
Awawa...\nWh-what should I do, Uesugi-san?
Don't ask me...
Ah, I see!\nI'll stay in Uesugi-san's room%p-2;――
Did you say something, Yotsuba?
N-no, nothing!\nI mean, that's not too unreasonable, right?
Why do you two insist\non rooming alone in the first place?
It's because it's dangerous to let Nino stay alone.\nYou never know what she might do.
Oh, can't the same thing be said about YOU, Miku?
Okay, that's enough.\nIf we keep arguing like this,\nwe'll be causing trouble for the hotel.
Here, compromise, the two of you share a room.
...If you say so, Futaro.
Well, it's better than Miku\nhaving a single room.
Alright, it's settled.
What about the room you'll be staying in, Yotsuba?\nIt's going to be just you, is that okay?
Yep.\nIt's okay with me,\neven though I'll feel a little lonely.
Now that we've decided, let's check-in.
Hey, once we've done that,\nwanna go drop off our luggage and have dinner?\nIt's not like we're going sightseeing today.
This hotel's restaurant has a FANTASTIC reputation!
We haven't had a decent meal\nsince we arrived in Okinawa.\nWe're also hungry, so why not?
Fu-kun.\nFor dinner, we should have western food, right?
I think familiar Japanese food\nwould be good for you, Futaro.
H-here we go again.\nDon't so get close to me, I said...
It's up to you, Futaro-kun.\nBy the way, as for me...either is fine.
I agree.\nIf it fills me up, it doesn't matter which one.
Yup.
...Humf.
Well, I want to try both!\nLet's check the menu first before deciding.
That sounds good.\nThere's also a lounge and cafe, apparently.
On the other hand,\nthere are too many delicious-looking options.\nI wonder what to eat?
...Ichika.\nWe'll leave our luggage here, so please check us in.
Ah, okay.\nPlease take care of my luggage, too.
Okay, I've got yours, Ichika.\nItsuki, you have a lot of luggage, don't you?\nShall I?
That would be great.\nThank you very much.
(...Even though it's about food,\n Itsuki isn't showing any interest, which is unusual.)
(She also seems like the type to be the one \n mediating who's rooming where.)
(Is Itsuki okay?\n She seems strangely unhappy.)
Uesugi-san?\nIs something wrong?
Ah, if you're tired,\nI'll carry your luggage too.
Nah, I'm good.
(Now this...\n is starting to feel like \n a more difficult trip than I thought.)
